verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is a single-pass transmembrane protein and member of the EGF ligand family. It is synthesized as a membrane-anchored precursor (TM-anchored ectodomain) that can be shed by ADAM proteases to release a soluble active form. The TM-anchored precursor stage is documented on the cell surface by surface biotinylation and flow cytometry, and the protein functions both as a juxtacrine signaling molecule in its membrane-bound state and as a paracrine ligand after regulated ectodomain shedding. The membrane-anchored precursor is a stable, bona-fide surface form with documented residence on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane, making it accessible to surface-directed binders. Although shedding produces a soluble fragment, the TM precursor is the canonical anchored form and qualifies as a classical surface receptor.
